beats.text: Almost every real machine learning failure happens outside the model, in the surrounding configuration, data, serving, and monitoring code.

visual.sub: ML code is a small box in a large system

beats.text: In the Sculley diagram the ML code is a few percent of the system, dwarfed by the infrastructure around it.
A run is reproducible only if fixing the data, code, config, environment, and seed fixes the output exactly.

visual.note: Drop any one input and you have a story, not a result

beats.text: The asset you ship is not the weights file, it is the versioned recipe that regenerates the weights file.
Content-addressed stages let a pipeline skip whatever did not change and rerun only what is downstream of an edit.
Maturity climbs a ladder from manual notebooks to governed monitoring, and you do not get to skip rungs.
Two systems sit at the heart of any serious ML platform, and they answer two very different questions.

visual.sub: What did we try, and what is live right now

beats.text: An experiment tracker logs the parameters, metrics, data hash, and commit of every run so past results stay searchable.
A model registry is a state machine over versions, moving none to staging to production to archived through gated transitions.

visual.note: Each arrow is a guarded, recorded transition

beats.text: The one invariant that matters: at most one version is production per deployment slot, and you can name it in a single query.
Because full lineage is attached, rolling back is just re-pointing the serving slot at an already validated artifact.
In 2026 the tracker and registry are often one platform, and a model version is increasingly a tuple of base model, adapter, and tools.
Software CI/CD tests code, but ML CI/CD must also test data and models, since any of the three can break production.

visual.sub: Gates for data, model, and infrastructure

beats.text: The ML Test Score runs three layers of gates: tests for the data, tests for the model, and tests for the infrastructure.
A challenger is promoted only if it beats the champion by a real margin and breaks no guardrail metric.

visual.note: The challenger is presumed guilty until it earns its place

beats.text: On a frozen holdout of two thousand rows at ninety percent accuracy, the promotion margin must clear about thirteen thousandths.
Continuous training retrains on fresh data, but automated retraining never means automated deployment, since the gate stays in the loop.
Editing one stage dirties everything downstream while upstream stages stay cached, so iteration is cheap.
A deployed model decays even though its weights never change, because the world the weights describe keeps moving.
Data drift moves the input distribution, while concept drift changes the relationship between inputs and the right answer.

visual.note: Only concept drift necessarily degrades accuracy

beats.text: Labels arrive late, so the first defence is the Population Stability Index comparing baseline against a recent window.
The industry rule of thumb reads PSI as stable, moderate, or significant across the tenth and quarter thresholds.
One bucket shifting from twenty to thirty percent contributes four hundredths to the total PSI score.
Behind every alert sits lineage, which tells you which model is responsible, what it trained on, and what to roll back to.
Governance is the layer that makes engineering controls accountable: who may deploy, who signed off, and what evidence exists.
SR 11-7 frames model risk as two sources at once: a model can be wrong, and a good model can be misused.

visual.tex: \text{Model risk} = P(\text{model is wrong}) + P(\text{model is misused})

beats.text: The guidance prescribes three pillars: robust development, independent validation, and governance with policies and controls.
The same controls reappear across the EU AI Act, the NIST framework, and ISO forty-two thousand and one.
Mature organisations keep five governance artifacts, from the model inventory to the monitoring and incident log.
The defensible middle is risk-tiered governance, matching the weight of controls to the stakes of the decision.
